page: Microsoft Azure E96ds_v7 is an Edsv7-family virtual machine configured with 96 dedicated vCPUs and 768.0 GB of system memory. Operating on the x86_64 architecture with Intel processors, this server type utilizes the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor to deliver dedicated CPU allocation. The memory configuration provides 8.0 GB of RAM per core, establishing a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio.

This instance features 5669 GB of local storage and is premium storage capable, allowing integration with high-performance external disk options. It does not include any integrated GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

The high memory-to-vCPU ratio and large local storage capacity provide resource density for memory-intensive applications. This hardware profile is designed for workloads such as large relational databases, in-memory caching systems, and enterprise resource planning applications that require substantial memory capacity alongside significant local storage.
description: Microsoft Azure E96ds_v7 is an Edsv7-family virtual machine featuring 96 dedicated vCPUs and 768.0 GB of RAM, running on an x86_64 Intel processor architecture via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. With a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, this server is designed for memory-intensive workloads. It includes 5669 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage, though it lacks integrated GPUs and complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The high resource density of this instance provides qualitative cost efficiency for enterprise applications that demand substantial memory and local disk space. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent compute performance without resource contention. It is suited for large-scale databases, in-memory data stores, and data warehousing workloads.

tagline: A high-density memory-optimized virtual machine featuring ninety-six dedicated cores, substantial RAM, and extensive local storage capacity.
